chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1011 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Present: Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Mr. Justice Abdul Hamid Bhurgri 06.03.2025 None present for the petitioner O R D E R ZULFIQAR ALI SANGI. J.- Through instant Constitutional Petition, the petitioner seeks direction against the Respondents to constitute Super Medical Board for reexamining the petitioner/injured, namely Tufail Ahmed Rajput. Since Counsel for the petitioner is called absent, therefore, we have perused the material available record. Perusal of record reflects that in the first instance the injured was referred to the RHC, Mithiyani, District Naushahro Feroze, where he was examined by Medical Officer and such provisional medico-legal certificate was issued and after receipt of Radiological opinion the final medico-legal certificate was also issued to the injured/petitioner. Record further reflects that the private respondents/accused being dissatisfied with the opinion of the Medical Officer, RHC Mithiyani moved an application for constituting Special Medical Board. Such Special Medical Board was constituted where the injured Tufail Ahmed was reexamined and vide Letter No:PUMHSW/SBA/MB/3814 dated 20.05.2024 such opinion was issued. The petitioner/injured being aggrieved of the opinion of the Special Medical Board moved an application Page 2 of 2 to respondent No.2/Director General Health Services Sindh, Hyderabad for constitution of Super Medical Board. Since the Constitution of Super Medical Board has been abolished in view of the order dated 16th May, 2006 w.e.f. 1st.

The petitioner/injured being aggrieved of the opinion of the Special Medical Board moved an application Page 2 of 2 to respondent No.2/Director General Health Services Sindh, Hyderabad for constitution of Super Medical Board. Since the Constitution of Super Medical Board has been abolished in view of the order dated 16th May, 2006 w.e.f. 1st. June, 2006 by the Health Department, Government of Sindh, therefore, no case for indulgence is made out. Consequently, instant petition being not maintainable is hereby dismissed in limine. Judge Judge ARBROHI
